title: On the Illusion of Progress
author: From: The Myth of Sisyphus by Albert Camus
content: The struggle itself toward the heights is enough to fill a man's heart. One must imagine Sisyphus happy, because he finds meaning in the action itself, not just the summit.
action: Action: Find a task you find repetitive and redefine it as a 'mastery exercise' rather than a chore.

title: The Tyranny of the Urgent
author: From: Charles Hummel
content: The urgent is rarely important, and the important is rarely urgent. We must manage the tension between the two.
action: Action: Review your calendar and delete one 'urgent' meeting that doesn't advance your primary mission.

title: The Pygmalion Effect
author: From: Behavioral Psychology
content: Expectations dictate performance. Higher expectations placed on oneself or others lead to higher performance outcomes.
action: Action: Set one 'reach' goal for a task you usually perform at a 'maintenance' level.

title: The Curse of Knowledge
author: From: Made to Stick
content: Once we know something, we find it nearly impossible to imagine what it was like not to know it, which ruins communication.
action: Action: Explain a complex concept to someone outside your field, stripping away all jargon.

title: Strategic Patience
author: From: The Art of War
content: He who knows when he can fight and when he cannot will be victorious. Patience is a weapon.
action: Action: Identify a situation where the best strategic move is to 'wait and see' rather than act.
